title: Y2K: How the 2000s Became Everything (essays on the future that never was)
author: Colette Shade

title: The Metamorphosis & Other Stories
author: Franz Kafka
comments: I have no doubt in my mind that The Metamorphosis itself is his magnum opus, but it was really interesting reading the other stories in this compilation.. I feel there's a 50% chance I would get along with Kafka, from his stories, I feel he would be a wildcard. Maybe 30%. He would be interesting to talk to.

title: Beautiful Music for Ugly Children
author: Kirstin Cronn-Mills
comments: I liked the part when they mentioned the Decemberists, and the rest of the book too I guess.... an easier read but I enjoyed it. i cried too

title: Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
comments: I didn't know this book was a 'trilogy', I thought it was just one book for the longest time..

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
comments: I've been told the best way to read Leaves of Grass is aloud.. I'll need to try that out sometime

title: Enlightenment Now
author: Steven Pinker
comments: Steven Pinker is a terrible person do not buy his books. The book itself was alright I just wished I didn't give him money

title: I Deliver Parcels in Beijing
author: Hu Anyan
comments: I need to write a blog about this book

title: Socialism: Uptopian and Scientific
author: Friedrich Engels
comments: kind of boring. sorry for the reductive take...

title: The Communist Manifesto
author: Karl Marx
comments: made sure to read this book out loud on public transport with a copy that had a hammer and sickle on the front <- is this funny

title: Fun Home
author: Alison Bechdel
comments: Read this for an lgbtq literature class, and absolutely loved it

title: The Well of Loneliness
author: Radclyffe Hall
comments: also read this for an lgbtq literature class. I want to reread eventually (probably will end up being so long from now) since when I first read it I was under the time constraint of the class. idk it's also just a good book

title: Imre: A Memorandum
author: Edward Prime-Stevenson
comments: Also!!! read this for an lgbtq literature class. I don't have much to say about Imre. This is the only book on here I don't have the hard copy of!
